purpose: Record the fixed court-coordinate interpretation used for all new labels and exports.
confirmed_geometry: K1/K6 and K11/K16 are the four real baseline/sideline court corners; no basket-center landmark is present.
Each baseline orders its six trained features from one outer corner through three-point and lane junctions to the other outer corner.
K7 and K8 are the two midcourt-line/sideline intersections; no center-circle landmark is present.
K9/K10 and K17/K18 are the lane/free-throw-line corners.
orientation_rule: Review of representative frames from clips 001–004 found the basket-to-basket court axis is left-to-right in footage. North remains top in the canonical diagram, but is the image-left basket on a clip's locked anchor frame. Fresh raw model output is normalized once per clip with either the identity or 180-degree permutation in court_keypoints.v2.json. Saved labels are already canonical and are never normalized again.
evidence: The v2.1 map restores the trained raw-landmark order: six real baseline features at each end, two real sideline/midcourt intersections, and two lane/free-throw corners at each end. The 180-degree permutation is the direct counterpart swap for every listed normalization pair. Labels from schema 2.0.0 are incompatible and must be relabeled.
